steps.title: Focus on noun behavior
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between Bengali and Danish nouns. The useful contrast is that Bengali changes the noun form for location, while Danish keeps the noun unchanged and uses a preposition.

steps.title: Reject family-based contrasts
steps.content: Choice B compares language families only. Choice C mentions Bengali noun change but shifts to language family. Choice D also turns the contrast toward Indo-Aryan versus Germanic instead of how Danish nouns mark location.

steps.title: State the noun contrast
steps.content: A suffix added to a Bengali noun contrasts directly with an unchanged Danish noun plus a preposition, which is the difference the notes identify. The answer is A.
